Ang CvSU ay nagsimula noong 1906 bilang Indang Intermediate School, sa panahon ng mga Thomasites.

Naging Indang Farm School ito noong 1918, Indang Rural High School noong 1927, Don Severino National Agriculture School noong 1958, at naging State College noong 1964 sa bisa ng RA 3917.

Noong Enero 22, 1998, sa bisa ng RA 8468, ito ay naging Cavite State University.

Noong 2001, ang CACOF (Naic) at CCAT (Rosario) ay isinama sa CvSU sa pamamagitan ng CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000.

Sa kasalukuyan, may 11 campus ang CvSU sa lalawigan; ang Main Campus (Don Severino delas Alas Campus, humigit-kumulang 70 ektarya) ay nasa Indang, Cavite.

May halos 100 curricular programs at mahigit 25,000 estudyante ang Pamantasan, at ipinagdiwang nito ang ika-120 anibersaryo ng pagkakatatag noong Enero 2026.

Pinagmulan: https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/ Ang pangulo ng CvSU ay si Dr. Ma.

Ang Main Campus ay ipinangalan kay Don Severino de las Alas — isang guro, lingkod-bayan, at pilantropo na nagkaloob ng bahagi ng kanyang lupain para sa paaralang naging CvSU.

question: Ano ang core values ng CvSU na binanggit sa 120th anniversary celebration?
gold_answer: Ang core values ng CvSU ay Truth, Integrity, Excellence, and Service, na siyang paggabay sa commitment nito sa quality education at nation-building.

response.text:
Per the official University page, CvSU is mandated under Section 2 of Republic Act No. 8468 to "primarily provide advance instruction and professional training in agriculture, science and technology, education and other related fields, undertake research and extension services, and provide progressive leadership in these areas." Its vision is "The premier university in historic Cavite globally recognized for excellence in character development, academics, research, innovation and sustainable community engagement," and its mission states that "Cavite State University shall provide excellent, equitable and relevant educational opportunities in the arts, sciences and technology through quality instruction and responsive research and development activities.

It shall produce professional, skilled and morally upright individuals for global competitiveness." The University's core values are Truth, Integrity, Excellence, and Service, and its quality policy commits to the highest standards of education, values its stakeholders, strives for continual improvement of its products and services, and upholds the University's tenets of Truth, Excellence, and Service to produce globally competitive and morally upright individuals.

Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/mandate-mission-vision-core-values-quality-policy/ The University's current strategy is CvSU ASCEND — Advancing Sustained Change and Excellence for National Development — its 2025-2028 Strategic Framework, implemented through the four-year IDEAL Plan: Inclusive and Accessible Education; Dynamic and Competitive Research and Innovation; Empowered Communities and Stronger Partnership; Accountable and Client-Centered Governance; and Long-Lasting Sustainable Resource Generation — as CvSU continues its journey toward becoming a Premier Global University.

response.text:
Cavite State University (CvSU) traces its roots to 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School, with the American Thomasites as the first teachers.

It was renamed Indang Farm School in 1918, Indang Rural High School in 1927, and Don Severino National Agriculture School in 1958.

In 1964, the school was converted into a State College via Republic Act 3917 and became known as Don Severino Agricultural College (DSAC).

On January 22, 1998, by virtue of Republic Act No. 8468, DSAC was converted into Cavite State University.

In 2001, the Cavite College of Fisheries (CACOF) in Naic and the Cavite College of Arts and Trade (CCAT) in Rosario were integrated into the University via CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000.

At present, CvSU has 11 campuses in the province; the main campus — the Don Severino delas Alas Campus in Indang, Cavite — is approximately 70 hectares in land area.

The University offers close to 100 curricular programs in the undergraduate and graduate levels, with more than 25,000 students and 1,200 faculty and staff from all campuses.

CvSU celebrated its 120th founding anniversary in January 2026.

Source:
- https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/ The main campus is named after Don Severino de las Alas — an educator
- public servant
- philanthropist who donated a portion of his land for the establishment of the intermediate school that became CvSU

The University is led by President Dr. Ma.

Agnes P.

Nuestro.

It is accredited by TESDA as a Trade and Testing Venue, designated by the Department of Agriculture as the National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture, and hosts the Southern Tagalog Agriculture Research and Development Consortium (STARDEC), the Affiliated Renewable Energy Center for Region IV-A, and the Regional ICT Center for Region IV-A.

CvSU holds AACCUP Institutional Accreditation Level III (recognized as the second top performing state university with the highest number of Level III accredited programs), is ISO 9001:2015 certified, ranked 217th in the WURI 2026 Top 500 Global Innovative Universities, and recently received its 5th IPOPHL Platinum Award and first Palladium Award — the first higher education institution in the Southern Tagalog Region to do so — plus top eco-friendly school honors from the DENR-EMB.

Cavite State University (CvSU) traces its roots to 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School, with the American Thomasites as the first teachers.

It was renamed Indang Farm School in 1918, Indang Rural High School in 1927, and Don Severino National Agriculture School in 1958.

Cavite State University (CvSU) traces its roots to 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School, with the American Thomasites as the first teachers.

It was renamed Indang Farm School in 1918, Indang Rural High School in 1927, and Don Severino National Agriculture School in 1958.

In 1964, the school was converted into a State College via Republic Act 3917 and became known as Don Severino Agricultural College (DSAC).

On January 22, 1998, by virtue of Republic Act No. 8468, DSAC was converted into Cavite State University.

In 2001, the Cavite College of Fisheries (CACOF) in Naic and the Cavite College of Arts and Trade (CCAT) in Rosario were integrated into the University via CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000.

At present, CvSU has 11 campuses in the province; the main campus — the Don Severino delas Alas Campus in Indang, Cavite — is approximately 70 hectares in land area.

The University offers close to 100 curricular programs in the undergraduate and graduate levels, with more than 25,000 students and 1,200 faculty and staff from all campuses.

CvSU celebrated its 120th founding anniversary in January 2026.

Source:
- https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/ The main campus is named after Don Severino de las Alas — an educator
- public servant
- philanthropist who donated a portion of his land for the establishment of the intermediate school that became CvSU

The University is led by President Dr. Ma.

Agnes P.

Nuestro.

It is accredited by TESDA as a Trade and Testing Venue, designated by the Department of Agriculture as the National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture, and hosts the Southern Tagalog Agriculture Research and Development Consortium (STARDEC), the Affiliated Renewable Energy Center for Region IV-A, and the Regional ICT Center for Region IV-A.

CvSU holds AACCUP Institutional Accreditation Level III (recognized as the second top performing state university with the highest number of Level III accredited programs), is ISO 9001:2015 certified, ranked 217th in the WURI 2026 Top 500 Global Innovative Universities, and recently received its 5th IPOPHL Platinum Award and first Palladium Award — the first higher education institution in the Southern Tagalog Region to do so — plus top eco-friendly school honors from the DENR-EMB.

question: What are the core values of CvSU?
gold_answer: CvSU's four core values are Truth, Integrity, Excellence, and Service.

response.text:
Per the official University page, CvSU is mandated under Section 2 of Republic Act No. 8468 to "primarily provide advance instruction and professional training in agriculture, science and technology, education and other related fields, undertake research and extension services, and provide progressive leadership in these areas." It shall produce professional, skilled and morally upright individuals for global competitiveness." The University's core values are Truth, Integrity, Excellence, and Service, and its quality policy commits to the highest standards of education, values its stakeholders, strives for continual improvement of its products and services, and upholds the University's tenets of Truth, Excellence, and Service to produce globally competitive and morally upright individuals.

In 1964, the school was converted into a State College via Republic Act 3917 and became known as Don Severino Agricultural College (DSAC).

On January 22, 1998, by virtue of Republic Act No. 8468, DSAC was converted into Cavite State University.

In 2001, the Cavite College of Fisheries (CACOF) in Naic and the Cavite College of Arts and Trade (CCAT) in Rosario were integrated into the University via CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000.

At present, CvSU has 11 campuses in the province; the main campus — the Don Severino delas Alas Campus in Indang, Cavite — is approximately 70 hectares in land area.

The University offers close to 100 curricular programs in the undergraduate and graduate levels, with more than 25,000 students and 1,200 faculty and staff from all campuses.

CvSU celebrated its 120th founding anniversary in January 2026.

Source:
- https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/ The main campus is named after Don Severino de las Alas — an educator
- public servant
- philanthropist who donated a portion of his land for the establishment of the intermediate school that became CvSU

The University is led by President Dr. Ma.

Agnes P.

Nuestro.

It is accredited by TESDA as a Trade and Testing Venue, designated by the Department of Agriculture as the National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture, and hosts the Southern Tagalog Agriculture Research and Development Consortium (STARDEC), the Affiliated Renewable Energy Center for Region IV-A, and the Regional ICT Center for Region IV-A.

CvSU holds AACCUP Institutional Accreditation Level III (recognized as the second top performing state university with the highest number of Level III accredited programs), is ISO 9001:2015 certified, ranked 217th in the WURI 2026 Top 500 Global Innovative Universities, and recently received its 5th IPOPHL Platinum Award and first Palladium Award — the first higher education institution in the Southern Tagalog Region to do so — plus top eco-friendly school honors from the DENR-EMB.

(Rankings, board-exam results, and accreditation levels cited here are as of 2026 — verify the latest figures at https://cvsu.edu.ph.)
